response.justification: The candidate brings genuine geospatial domain exposure through founding GISCollective and building web GIS applications, plus low-latency distributed systems experience at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic handling millions of requests per second. However, the role's primary requirement is modern C++ (or Rust), and his C++ experience is brief and dated (a six-month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015), with his systems work done mostly in DLang and his recent years focused on TypeScript/Node.js product work. He has no direct navigation or routing pipeline experience, making him an adjacent rather than direct fit.
strengths: Founded and operates a geospatial data platform (GISCollective), showing real GIS domain knowledge
Low-latency, high-throughput distributed systems experience (async I/O, caching, fibers) at ad-tech scale
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, Linux, Kubernetes, and open-source contributions
concerns: Modern C++ experience is minimal and roughly a decade old; no Rust experience listed
No direct experience with routing engines, map tiling, or navigation guidance systems
Recent roles (2020+) are web/full-stack TypeScript work, distant from systems-level pipeline engineering
